sections.title: Performance Analysis
sections.content:
No React in this project (SvelteKit, not yet built). Skipping that section. Issues found across backend + engine.

---

sections.title: 1. Race Condition / Inconsistent State (Critical)
sections.content:
**`websocket.py:38-54` and `games.py:45-83`** â€” `get â†’ mutate â†’ save` is not atomic. Two simultaneous WebSocket clients submit orders concurrently: both `get()` the same state, both save, one overwrites the other.

`game_store.get()` has no lock; only `save()` does. The per-operation lock is insufficient.

**Fix:** lock the entire transaction:

```python

sections.title: game_store.py
sections.content:
async def transaction(self, game_id: str):
    """Context manager that locks + loads state, saves on exit."""
    # Use asyncio.Lock per game_id instead of one global lock
```

Or simpler: replace `_lock` with a per-game lock dict:

```python
class InMemoryGameStore:
    def __init__(self):
        self._games: dict[str, GameState] = {}
        self._locks: dict[str, asyncio.Lock] = {}

    def _lock_for(self, game_id: str) -> asyncio.Lock:
        if game_id not in self._locks:
            self._locks[game_id] = asyncio.Lock()
        return self._locks[game_id]
```

Then websocket handler:

```python
async with game_store._lock_for(game_id):
    state = await game_store.get(game_id)
    state = engine.submit_order(state, player_role, quantity)
    if state.is_round_complete():
        state = engine.process_round(state)
    await game_store.save(state)
```

---

sections.title: 2. Memory Leak: `incoming_orders` Grows Unbounded
sections.content:
**`core/models/game.py:35`** â€” `incoming_orders: list[int]` appended every round in `order_processor.py:72`, never trimmed. After 26 rounds with 4 players, each `PlayerState` has 26 entries accumulating for the life of the game.

**Fix in `order_processor.py`:** trim after consuming:

```python
upstream_ps.incoming_orders.append(order_qty)

sections.title: Or: only keep last N orders (for display/history purposes)
sections.content:
```

If `incoming_orders` is only used for display, cap it:

```python
upstream_ps.incoming_orders = upstream_ps.incoming_orders[-10:] + [order_qty]
```

Same issue with `customer_demand_history` in `game.py:53` â€” unbounded list, never read back in engine logic.

---

sections.title: 3. Redundant `game_store.get()` Calls
sections.content:
**`websocket.py:38` and `:69`** â€” state fetched again after already being in scope:

```python

sections.title: Line 38: redundant fetch â€” state already loaded at line 19
sections.content:
state = await game_store.get(game_id)
```

Same pattern in `games.py:45`. Each `get()` hits the dict + async overhead.

**Fix:** pass `state` through the transaction scope, fetch once.

---

sections.title: 4. Unnecessary Object Allocation in `advance_shipments`
sections.content:
**`order_processor.py:9-13`** â€” creates new `IncomingShipment` objects for every in-flight shipment every round, even ones just needing a timer decrement:

```python

sections.title: Current: allocates N new Pydantic objects per round
sections.content:
ps.incoming_shipments = [
    IncomingShipment(quantity=s.quantity, arrives_in_rounds=s.arrives_in_rounds - 1)
    for s in ps.incoming_shipments
    if s.arrives_in_rounds > 1
]
```

`PlayerState` uses Pydantic `BaseModel` (immutable by default). Use `model_copy`:

```python
ps.incoming_shipments = [
    s.model_copy(update={"arrives_in_rounds": s.arrives_in_rounds - 1})
    for s in ps.incoming_shipments
    if s.arrives_in_rounds > 1
]
```

Or declare `IncomingShipment` as a plain dataclass with `__slots__` â€” no Pydantic overhead for internal engine objects. Ship Pydantic only at API boundaries.

---

sections.title: 5. `OrderUpToAI` Instantiated Fresh Every Round
sections.content:
**`websocket.py:49`, `games.py:69`** â€” `OrderUpToAI(r)` constructed inside the AI-fill loop every round. Stateless object, zero reason to recreate it.

```python

sections.title: Current (wasteful):
6. `total_cost()` â€” No Caching
sections.content:
**`game.py:59`** â€” recomputes sum across all players on every call. Called in `broadcast()` after every round. With 4 players it's trivial now, but variant engines may add dozens of roles.

**Fix:** accumulate in `accrue_costs()`:

```python

sections.title: In accrue_costs():
sections.content:
cost = ...
ps.cumulative_cost += cost
state.total_cumulative_cost += cost  # maintain running total
```

`total_cost()` becomes `return self.total_cumulative_cost`.

---

sections.title: 7. Future N+1 Risk (PostgreSQL transition)
sections.content:
No DB yet, but `game_store.get()` is called once per WebSocket message. When the store swaps to PostgreSQL:
- Each `order` action = 1 SELECT + 1 UPDATE minimum
- `broadcast()` fan-out to 4 players = 4 separate sends with no batching

**Pre-empt it:** use Redis pub/sub for broadcast (already in CLAUDE.md stack). Redis pipeline the SELECT+UPDATE as a Lua script or `WATCH/MULTI/EXEC` for atomicity. Don't wait until PostgreSQL causes slowdowns to design this.

---

sections.title: Summary Table
sections.content:
| Issue | File | Impact | Fix effort |
|-------|------|--------|------------|
| Race condition on orders | `websocket.py`, `game_store.py` | High â€” data loss | Medium |
| `incoming_orders` memory leak | `order_processor.py:72` | Medium â€” grows each round | Low |
| Redundant `game_store.get()` | `websocket.py:38`, `games.py:45` | Low-Medium | Low |
| Pydantic object churn in shipments | `order_processor.py:9-13` | Low now, high at scale | Low |
| AI re-instantiation per round | `websocket.py:49`, `games.py:69` | Low | Trivial |
| `total_cost()` recompute | `game.py:59` | Low now | Low |

The race condition is the only one that causes incorrect behavior today. Rest are scale/cleanliness issues.

    """Context manager that locks + loads state, saves on exit."""
    # Use asyncio.Lock per game_id instead of one global lock

    state = await game_store.get(game_id)
    state = engine.submit_order(state, player_role, quantity)
    if state.is_round_complete():
        state = engine.process_round(state)
    await game_store.save(state)

    IncomingShipment(quantity=s.quantity, arrives_in_rounds=s.arrives_in_rounds - 1)
    for s in ps.incoming_shipments

    for s in ps.incoming_shipments
    if s.arrives_in_rounds > 1

Got enough. Here's the analysis.

---
